description_long: This is a large, amphora-style vase with a wide, flared mouth and a rounded body tapering to a narrow base. The vase features two prominent, curved handles on either side, which are attached at the shoulder and upper body. Its surface is richly decorated with classical Greco-Roman motifs, including detailed painted scenes of human figures and mythological creatures in earthy tones of red, black, and white. The artwork wraps around the body, with intricate borders and ornamental patterns near the base and neck. The finish appears glossy, suggesting a ceramic glaze, and the overall craftsmanship is refined, evoking the look of an ancient artifact. This vase would serve as a striking decorative piece in a public or formal urban setting.
description: A large, amphora-style vase with ornate handles and classical painted scenes in red, black, and white, finished with a glossy ceramic glaze.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from an angle highlighting one handle and the flared mouth, with mythological figures visible on the body.
description_view_1: This view shows both handles symmetrically and provides a clear look at the main painted scene with multiple human figures.
description_view_2: The vase is rotated to display the opposite handle and a less detailed section of the painted motif, with the neck and base clearly visible.
description_view_3: Both handles are visible, and the main mythological scene is prominent, emphasizing the decorative artwork and glossy finish.
attribute_pattern: classical figurative motifs with ornamental borders
